Title: User Registration with Valid Credentials
Test Data:
- Email: test@example.com
- Password: Test123
- Name: John Doe
- Nationality: United States
- Role: User
Test Steps:
1. User enters registration data into the UI.
2. UI sends the registration data to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication validates the data.
4. FirebaseAuthentication returns a valid token and confirms the user's save.
5. UI saves the user data.
6. UI confirms the successful registration.
Expected Result: User is registered successfully and receives a confirmation message.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Credentials
Test Data:
- Email: invalidemail
- Password: short
- Name: Jane Doe
- Nationality: United Kingdom
- Role: User
Test Steps:
1. User enters registration data into the UI.
2. UI sends the registration data to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication returns an error message.
4. UI displays the error message to the user.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: User Login with Valid Credentials
Test Steps:
1. User enters login credentials into the UI.
2. UI sends the credentials to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication validates the credentials.
4. FirebaseAuthentication returns valid credentials and a token.
5. UI receives the valid credentials and token.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully and receives a confirmation message.

Title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
Test Steps:
1. User enters login credentials into the UI.
2. UI sends the credentials to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication returns an error message.
4. UI displays the error message to the user.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: User Creates a Report with Valid Data
Test Data:
- Description: "Tree fallen on sidewalk"
- Image: (image file)
- Coordinates: (latitude, longitude)
- Urgency: High
- Category: Tree Maintenance
- Area: Central Park
Test Steps:
1. User selects "Create Report" from the UI.
2. User enters report details including description, image, coordinates, urgency, category, and area.
3. UI uploads the image to FirebaseStorage.
4. UI sends the report data to FirebaseFirestore.
5. FirebaseFirestore searches for the nearest competent entity based on coordinates and urgency.
6. FirebaseFirestore returns the nearest entity data.
7. UI saves the report with the image URL and entity data.
8. UI confirms the report saving.
Expected Result: Report is created successfully and assigned to a competent entity, with a confirmation message displayed to the user.

Title: User Creates a Report with Invalid Data
Test Data:
- Description: ""
- Image: (invalid image file)
- Coordinates: (invalid latitude, longitude)
- Urgency: Low
- Category: Invalid Category
- Area: Invalid Area
Test Steps:
1. User selects "Create Report" from the UI.
2. User enters report details including description, image, coordinates, urgency, category, and area.
3. UI attempts to upload the image to FirebaseStorage.
4. UI sends the report data to FirebaseFirestore.
5. FirebaseFirestore returns an error message.
6. UI displays the error message to the user.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid report data.

Title: User Receives a Notification
Preconditions: User is logged in and has pending reports.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in.
2. FirebaseFirestore checks for pending reports for the user.
3. FirebaseFirestore generates a notification for each pending report.
4. FirebaseFirestore sends the notification to the user.
5. UI displays the notification to the user.
Expected Result: User receives a notification for each pending report.

Title: User Does Not Receive a Notification
Preconditions: User is logged in but has no pending reports.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in.
2. FirebaseFirestore checks for pending reports for the user.
3. FirebaseFirestore finds no pending reports.
4. FirebaseFirestore does not generate any notifications.
5. UI does not display any notifications to the user.
Expected Result: User does not receive any notifications.

Title: Area Belongs to Category
Test Steps:
1. FirebaseFirestore checks if the area belongs to the specified category.
2. FirebaseFirestore confirms the relationship.
Expected Result: Relationship between the area and category is confirmed.

Title: Area Does Not Belong to Category
Test Steps:
1. FirebaseFirestore checks if the area belongs to the specified category.
2. FirebaseFirestore confirms the absence of the relationship.
Expected Result: Relationship between the area and category is not found.

Title: Category Has Reports
Test Steps:
1. FirebaseFirestore checks if the report belongs to the specified category.
2. FirebaseFirestore confirms the relationship.
Expected Result: Relationship between the report and category is confirmed.

Title: Category Does Not Have Reports
Test Steps:
1. FirebaseFirestore checks if the report belongs to the specified category.
2. FirebaseFirestore confirms the absence of the relationship.
Expected Result: Relationship between the report and category is not found.

Title: User Updates Profile Information

2. User navigates to the profile update section.

6. UI confirms the profile update.
Expected Result: User's profile information is updated successfully and confirmed.

Title: User Changes Password
Test Data:
- Current Password: Test123
- New Password: NewTest123
Test Steps:
1. User logs in.
2. User navigates to the password change section.
3. User enters the current password and new password.
4. UI sends the password change request to FirebaseAuthentication.
5. FirebaseAuthentication verifies the current password.
6. FirebaseAuthentication changes the password.
7. UI confirms the password change.
Expected Result: User's password is changed successfully and confirmed.
